what_it_does: Implements the SequenceWorkspace React view and its helper components: it renders a header, an interactive spine of state nodes, a selected-state detail panel, a references panel, a sequence-readiness rail, and an always-visible rejected-memory section. It reads global window.MS_DATA, tracks a selected state, and publishes the selection to window.ViContext.
observations: Reads the active sequence and its states from window.MS_DATA and sorts states by step.
Maintains selectedStateId with a fallback to states[3].id when no active state exists.
Publishes workspace, sequence, state, and mockup to window.ViContext inside a useEffect.
Renders decorative Sparkle overlays at fixed percentages along the spine.
StateNode switches UI by status: missing shows a plus/generate trigger, generating shows a spinner and review button, approved shows a canonical badge.
SelectedStatePanel shows either an approved mockup with metadata or generating/missing placeholders.
ReferencesPanel filters mockups with status 'style_anchor' and shows a hardcoded Vi rationale note.
ReadinessPanel computes approved/missing/generating counts but renders mostly hardcoded readiness rows and downstream package chips.
standouts: Decorative sparkle overlay on the sequence spine.
Status-driven node and panel rendering across the design-state lifecycle.
ViContext integration and Vi note for AI collaborator context.
conventions: Functional React components with destructured props.
Global singleton data via window.MS_DATA and context via window.ViContext.
CSS custom properties used for spacing, colors, radii, and typography.
Conditional action buttons based on state status.
Inline styles mixed with utility classes.
concerns: selectedStateId fallback to states[3].id will throw if the sequence has fewer than four states.
Readiness rows and milestone labels are hardcoded rather than derived from states data.
Sparkle positions, reference rationale, and generating candidate counts are hardcoded.
ReferencesPanel receives a state prop but never uses it.
Inline @keyframes spin and twinkle are repeated in multiple places.
Direct mutation of window.ViContext assumes a specific external contract not shown in the file.
notes: A status-driven sequence dashboard that turns a list of design states into a navigable spine, detail panels, readiness progress, and a visible memory of rejected work.
questions: What is the exact contract and lifecycle of window.ViContext?
Where are Btn, Icon, Panel, MockThumb, Chip, ModelChip, and ViNote defined?
Is seq guaranteed to exist, and can states ever be empty or fewer than four?
How is mockup.model mapped to provider IDs beyond the simple startsWith('gpt') check?
What shape does data.rejectionPatterns have and is it always present?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file implements the sequence spine, selected-state hero, reference/readiness panels, and rejected-memory rail described in the header comment.
apparent_themes: Sequence as a linear spine of design states.
Lifecycle visibility for approved, generating, and missing states.
Human-AI collaboration through Vi notes and ViContext.
Canonical approval as a first-class status.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to create a project-space control center where a team can see the whole sequence at a glance, drill into a selected state, act on missing or generating work, and keep rejected attempts visible to inform future generation.
novelty: The combination of a decorative sparkle spine, an always-visible rejected-memory gallery, and a readiness rail with downstream package chips gives the workspace a distinct narrative and operational feel.
contribution: It contributes the sequence workspace view itself: the state-node spine, status-driven detail panels, readiness progress surface, and rejected-memory gallery, along with the pattern of publishing selection state to a global Vi context.
summary_for_embedding: This file implements the SequenceWorkspace React view and its subcomponents, rendering an interactive spine of design states, a selected-state detail panel, style-anchor references, a readiness progress rail, and an always-visible rejected-memory gallery using global window.MS_DATA and ViContext.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to provide a project-space dashboard for tracking and acting on the lifecycle of a design sequence while surfacing style references and rejected attempts to guide future generation.

symbols.does: Renders a single state card on the sequence spine with status-aware thumbnail, labels, and action triggers.
symbols.description: StateNode displays a sequence state as a node on the spine, including a status dot, step number, name, and subtext. It conditionally renders a generate plus button for missing states, a spinner and review button for generating states, and a canonical badge for approved states.

symbols.does: Renders a small animated gold dot overlay on the spine.
symbols.description: Sparkle creates an absolutely positioned decorative dot with a twinkle animation, placed at caller-specified x/y coordinates and delay to add visual rhythm above the sequence rail.

symbols.does: Renders the main detail panel for the currently selected state.
symbols.description: SelectedStatePanel shows the selected state's mockup, metadata chips, and notes when approved, or generating/missing placeholders with relevant call-to-action buttons based on the state status.

symbols.does: Renders a grid of style-anchor reference mockups plus an add placeholder.
symbols.description: ReferencesPanel pulls up to three mockups marked as style_anchor from window.MS_DATA, displays them as thumbnails, and includes a Vi note explaining the style rationale; it accepts but does not use a state prop.

symbols.does: Renders the sequence readiness summary with progress bar and milestone rows.
symbols.description: ReadinessPanel computes counts of approved, missing, and generating states, renders a progress bar, lists hardcoded readiness milestones with optional CTAs, and shows downstream package chips.

symbols.does: Renders a single readiness list item with icon, tone, detail, and optional CTA.
symbols.description: ReadinessRow is a small presentational component that displays an icon colored by tone, a label, an optional detail line, and an optional small button for actions like Generate or Review.

symbols.does: Renders the always-visible rejected mockups and anti-pattern cards section.
symbols.description: RejectedMemory surfaces rejected mockups with their reasons and a subset of rejection-pattern cards, keeping past failures visible to shape future design decisions.
